artifacts.content:
From: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
To: nominations@aa.org
Cc: rachel.kim@outlook.com
Date: February 10, 2026 8:47 PM
Subject: Follow‑up on A.A. World Services Board Résumé and Certification Form

Dear Nominating Committee,

Thank you again for inviting me to submit the additional materials for consideration. I wanted to confirm that I have completed the online certification form this evening. The form shows my electronic certification date as 2026-03-02, which matches the timeline you outlined for the current round of potential Class B trustee and service pool candidates.

In the narrative sections, I tried to give a fuller picture of my background beyond my current role as Director of Program Operations at New Horizons Community Recovery Center. I included some detail about my experience navigating the criminal justice system after my DWI arrest in October 2005 in Queens. The six‑month probation supervised out of the Queens County Probation Office, along with the mandate to attend outpatient treatment at Elmhurst Recovery Services three nights a week and bring signed A.A. attendance slips from meetings in Jackson Heights, was formative for me. Working with my public defender, Ms. Angela Ortiz, and a very skeptical probation officer taught me firsthand how well‑intentioned policies can unintentionally punish people who are genuinely trying to change, and it continues to shape how I think about A.A.’s relationships with courts and treatment centers.

For transparency, I also noted that I once submitted a proposal to AA Grapevine for a small paid diversity and inclusion training series for their staff. That proposal was reviewed but ultimately not approved, and no contract was executed.

Please let me know if you need any clarifications, additional documentation, or references.

In fellowship,

Michael Anthony Rivera
New York, NY
mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org
(917) 555‑2934

metadata.subject: Follow‑up on A.A. World Services Board Résumé and Certification Form

artifacts.content:
From: hr@pmi-nycchapter.org
To: Michael Rivera <m.rivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
Date: 2025-12-11 08:17 AM
Subject: PMP Renewal Confirmation & PDU Audit (No Action Needed)

Hi Michael,

Thank you for submitting your Project Management Professional (PMP) renewal application through PMI.org.

This message confirms that your certification status has been extended for another three-year cycle. Your new cycle dates are **April 1, 2025 through March 31, 2028**, contingent on continued adherence to PMI’s Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct. The renewal is based on your completion of 60 PDUs logged between March 2022 and March 2025.

For your records, the renewal processing date was **March 29, 2025** and your digital certificate will be available for download within 7–10 business days. Please note that this is the only renewal notice you will receive from the NYC Chapter; future reminders will come directly from PMI.

We also completed a routine spot audit of a sample of your PDUs, including:
- “Trauma-Informed Project Management in Behavioral Health Settings” (webinar, 1.5 PDUs)
- “Nonprofit Budgeting for Program Directors” (live workshop, 3 PDUs)
- “Ethics in 12‑Step Fellowship Service and Professional Roles” (chapter event, 2 PDUs), where you discussed your experience coordinating an informal A.A. literature project to Rikers and Sing Sing and why your board declined to formalize the proposed $5,000 training contract.

All items were verified. No further documentation is required.

Congratulations on maintaining your PMP in good standing.

Best,

Angela Santos
Volunteer Certification Coordinator
PMI NYC Chapter

metadata.subject: PMP Renewal Confirmation & PDU Audit (No Action Needed)

artifacts.content:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@nycwell.org>
To: Michael Rivera <m.rivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
Date: 2024-02-07 09:42 PM
Subject: That DEI certificate and your "non‑degrees" :)

Hey Mike,

Just finished skimming that University of South Florida DEI in the Workplace certificate you printed and left on the kitchen table. The issue date says **May 18, 2021** and it still looks great in the frame, even if you keep joking that it’s “not a real degree.”

For the record, I’m glad you stuck with the shorter certificate programs after those two grad school false starts. You’ve told me a million times about starting public administration at Baruch and then social work at Hunter and never finishing either. Honestly, between taking extra shifts back at Urban Pathways so you could send Elena and Javier that $600/month for their co‑op fees, and then that high‑interest loan you took from Astoria Federal in October 2012, I don’t know how you would have survived full‑time grad school anyway.

Anyway, I think when you update your résumé you should list the USF DEI certificate with the actual completion date and maybe even mention that it’s come up in your work on the reentry program dashboards. It’s not just a line item; you actually use that stuff.

Love,

R.

P.S. Don’t forget to bring the certificate to the office on Friday so you can scan it for that funder portal—they were picky about uploading proof with the original issue dates.

Time: 3:00 PM – 4:00 PM
Location: Home Office – Upper West Side
Attendees: Michael Rivera

Description:

Block time to scan and upload professional certifications for New Horizons HR audit and upcoming grant applications.

Items to scan and label clearly with ISSUE DATES:

1. PMP Certificate

   - Last renewal processed: **March 29, 2025** (new cycle through March 31, 2028)

2. Columbia University SPS – Nonprofit Management Certificate
   - Completion date on certificate: **December 9, 2012**

3. USF – Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in the Workplace (online)

4. Various behavioral health and trauma‑informed care workshop certificates (2018–2024)

Remember to:

- Double‑check that no A.A. service roles are listed as “certifications.”
- For the ethics training where you talked about the 2013 group treasurer situation (missing ~$420 from the 7th Tradition basket and the repayment plan completed by December 2013), just confirm it’s categorized as “professional development,” not a formal credential.

Upload everything to: HR > Personnel Files > Rivera_Michael > Certifications.

From: training@nycbehavioralhealth.org
To: Michael Rivera <m.rivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
Date: 2023-10-02 01:26 PM
Subject: Confirmation – Trauma-Informed Care Certificate

Dear Mr. Rivera,

This email confirms successful completion of the “Trauma-Informed Care in Community Recovery Settings” intensive offered by the NYC Behavioral Health Training Collaborative.

Participant: Michael Anthony Rivera
Program Dates: September 18–22, 2023
Total Hours: 24 (meets NYS OASAS continuing education standards)

Your digital **Certificate of Completion** has been issued with an official date of **September 25, 2023**. Please reference this date when reporting continuing education hours to your employer or any credentialing bodies.

The certificate reflects your full legal name as entered above. If you notice any discrepancies, reply to this message by **October 16, 2023** so we can re‑issue. After that date, a $25 reprint fee applies.

We appreciate the way you integrated your occupational background into the case discussions, particularly your experience at Urban Pathways taking on extra evening intakes and Saturday groups while quietly supporting your sister and brother‑in‑law with their co‑op maintenance fees. Several participants commented that your examples made the financial‑stress aspect of trauma much more real.

You may download your certificate at any time from your learner portal under “My Credentials.”

Best regards,

Dana Lee, LCSW
Training Coordinator
NYC Behavioral Health Training Collaborative

Title: Deadline – Upload Certificates to AA Service Resume Draft
Date: 2025-06-07
Time: 10:30 AM – 11:30 AM
Location: New Horizons – Michael’s Office
Attendees: Michael Rivera

Description:

Use this hour to clean up the draft service résumé for the area committee.

Tasks:
- List all relevant professional certifications with their dates (PMP – original June 15, 2014; most recent renewal March 29, 2025; DEI certificate – May 18, 2021; Columbia Nonprofit Management – December 9, 2012; Trauma-Informed Care intensive – September 25, 2023).
- Make sure not to confuse A.A. service experience with “certifications.” The treasurer situation at West Side Serenity in early 2013 (discovering ~$420 missing from the 7th Tradition basket, helping set up a repayment plan completed by December 2013) belongs under “Experience with Group Conscience and Financial Principles,” not credentials.
- Clarify that the informal coordination you did with G.S.O.’s Corrections Desk (July 2020–February 2021) never turned into a paid contract and that you received no compensation.
- Add a brief note that you started but did not complete two graduate programs (MPA and MSW), emphasizing instead the certificates you finished.

Goal: Have the document ready to share with Thomas B. and the other past GSRs for feedback next week.

artifacts.content:
Subject: DEI Certificate – Confirmation of Completion & Date for Your Records

Hi Michael,

Congratulations again on completing the “Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in the Workplace” certificate through USF last year. Per your request, I’m confirming the official **completion and issuance date** for your HR and licensing files.

Our records show:

- Participant: Michael Anthony Rivera  
- Program: DEI in the Workplace (online)  
- Completion date: **2021-05-27**  
- Certificate issued: **2021-06-01**  
- Delivery method: electronic PDF via Canvas

If your organization’s credentialing or any grant application needs verification, they can reference the certificate ID on the bottom left of the PDF. If you’re filling out forms that ask for a “certification date,” they usually want the **issuance date (2021-06-01)** rather than the last class session.

You had mentioned in our Zoom that you failed the PMP exam the first time and then passed on your second attempt in 2014; if you’re listing multiple credentials, it might help to keep a simple table with each certification date so you’re not hunting through old emails.

Also, I appreciated what you shared about your experience volunteering with A.A. at Rikers Island and how that eventually led you to the District 10 Corrections Committee. The way you tied that into your current role at New Horizons was powerful and exactly the kind of real-world context we hope participants bring to the course.

Let me know if you need a reissued copy or a signed letter on USF letterhead.

Best,  
Caroline Ortiz  
Program Coordinator, Corporate Training  
University of South Florida (Online)

metadata.subject: DEI Certificate – Confirmation of Completion & Date for Your Records

artifacts.content:
Title: Block – Update Certification Dates in HR & Vendor Portals

Description:

Goal for this block: finally sit down and clean up all the **certification date** fields across HR, vendor portals, and funder applications.

To do:
- Log into PMI and confirm PMP **certification date** (I know I finally passed in 2014 after failing the first attempt, but I keep mixing up the exact month – pretty sure the pass letter was dated **2014-10-18**).  
- Pull the PDF for the Columbia SPS Nonprofit Management certificate and confirm the **issue date** (I think they stamped it **2012-06-30**, not the last class date).  
- Double‑check the USF DEI certificate completion date (believe it was late May 2021).  
- Make sure New Horizons’ internal training tracker uses the “issued” dates, not the dates I registered.

Notes:
- HR asked that anything with a lapse/renewal cycle have both original certification date and last renewal date.  
- For forms that ask “date of certification,” they specifically said to avoid putting today’s date or the date I’m filling out the form.  
- Also need to remove that abandoned idea about having New Horizons bill AA World Services for peer groups; we decided it would create a messy financial relationship with A.A. and doesn’t belong in any credentialing narrative.

Personal reminder: add a note about my early work history (Delgado & Ortiz Law Offices, 2002–2005) to the internal CV I use for grants – even though it ended badly, it’s still relevant experience.

Location: Home office – Upper West Side apartment

Duration: 2:00 p.m. – 3:30 p.m.

metadata.title: Block – Update Certification Dates in HR & Vendor Portals

artifacts.content:
Subject: Verification Letter – Urban Pathways BH Certificate & Start Date

Dear Mr. Rivera,

As requested, this email serves as verification of your employment and internal training history with Urban Pathways Behavioral Health Services in Brooklyn.

Our records indicate the following:

- Start date (part‑time, 20 hrs/week at $27/hr): **2010-08-09**  
- Transition to full‑time: **2011-02-07**  
- Completion of Urban Pathways Core Clinical & Recovery Practices Certificate: **2011-11-03**  
- Certificate issued: **2011-11-10**

For any forms that ask for a “certification date,” we generally advise staff to use the **issue date (2011-11-10)**. The training itself ran from September through early November 2011.

You had also asked whether we could briefly acknowledge the accommodations related to your probationary work schedule after your depressive episode and short‑term disability leave through BronxCare earlier in 2010. We can state that your initial part‑time schedule was recommended by your therapist and that you successfully transitioned to full‑time per that plan.

If you’re compiling a comprehensive CV for your current role at New Horizons Community Recovery Center, you may also want to note your earlier administrative experience at Delgado & Ortiz Law Offices (2002–2005), even though you mentioned there were performance write‑ups toward the end of that job.

Please let me know if you require this information on letterhead with a signature for credentialing or grant purposes.

Sincerely,  
Dr. Leonard Chang  
Program Director  
Urban Pathways Behavioral Health Services

metadata.subject: Verification Letter – Urban Pathways BH Certificate & Start Date

artifacts.content:
Title: Coffee w/ District 10 Old‑Timers – Talk Service & Credentials

Description:

Meet with Ray and “Big Joe” from District 10 to talk about next steps after my GSR rotation and how my professional certifications play into service work.

Agenda:
- Ask them how they list outside certifications (PMP, DEI, nonprofit management) when applying to speak at regional service events.  
- Clarify whether it’s appropriate to mention the exact **certification dates** on A.A. resumes or keep it more general (e.g., “PMP since 2014” instead of “certified on **2014-10-18**”).  
- Share my experience from the informal A.A. support circle at Rikers (2009–2011) and how that led to joining the Corrections Committee.  
- Own the fact that I’ve sometimes clashed with old‑timers about sticking to guidelines and that a couple of times I just stopped going to a group rather than dealing with conflict.

Notes to self:
- Don’t bring up that old idea I had about New Horizons billing AA World Services for peer groups unless they specifically ask about finances; that went nowhere and just confused people.  
- Bring copies of my DEI certificate (issued 2021-06-01) and the Columbia nonprofit certificate (issued 2012-06-30) so they can see how I’ve been listing the dates on applications.  
- Remember: they’re not the certification police; this is about being transparent and not overstating anything.

Location: Café Grumpy, 39th & Lex

Time: 7:30 p.m. – 9:00 p.m.

metadata.title: Coffee w/ District 10 Old‑Timers – Talk Service & Credentials

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
To: Admissions <info@urbanleadershipfellows.org>
Date: 2026-03-02 08:17 AM
Subject: Clarification on Application Materials and Address

Dear Selection Committee,

Thank you again for the opportunity to apply to the Urban Leadership Fellows program and for the helpful Q&A session last week. I’m writing to clarify a couple of items on my application before the March 5 deadline.

First, on the education section: I listed my B.A. in Sociology from Fordham (class of ’96), but I realized I didn’t explain a brief gap. I actually dropped out for a semester my junior year because my grades slipped and I was partying too much. That period was part of the progression that eventually led to my getting sober in 2007, and I wanted to be fully transparent about it.

Second, I want to confirm that you have my current home address correct for any mailed correspondence:

248 West 92nd Street, Apt 5C
New York, NY 10025

I’ve lived here on the Upper West Side for several years with my partner and her son, and it’s the most stable home base I’ve had.

Finally, in response to the question about prior A.A. and community service experience: during the Area 49 hybrid meetings ad hoc committee work in late 2020, I shared with a small planning group that my grandmother, Teresa Delgado, had contracted COVID-19 at her assisted living facility in the Bronx (St. Mary’s Assisted Living) in November 2020. I even passed along her room number to a fellow member so he could help test the Spanish-language audio stream to her room. That experience really shaped my commitment to equitable remote access.

If you need any additional documentation or clarification, I’m happy to provide it.

Best regards,

Michael Anthony Rivera
Director of Program Operations
New Horizons Community Recovery Center

metadata.subject: Clarification on Application Materials and Address

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
To: Leasing Office <info@riversideviewapartments.com>
Date: January 9, 2026 8:17 AM
Subject: Proof of Residence Letter for Assisted Living Application

Hi Denise,

Thanks again for agreeing to draft the proof-of-residence letter for my grandmother’s file. St. Mary’s Assisted Living in the Bronx is updating their records as part of the Medicaid spend‑down review I’ve been managing since her fall at Montefiore back in February 2022. Between reviewing those $3,000–$3,500 monthly statements and filing appeals with her geriatric social worker, Linda Perez, I feel like I live in paperwork these days.

For the letter, you can list my current mailing address as:

Michael Anthony Rivera
c/o New Horizons Community Recovery Center
Attn: Program Operations
415 West 45th Street, 6th Floor
New York, NY 10036

That’s the most reliable place for anything official to reach me during business hours, since I’m usually in Midtown juggling budgets and staff issues. I’ve actually turned down a couple of higher‑level executive offers at other agencies because I’m not sure I want the extra politics that come with sitting in the big chair, so New Horizons is still very much home base.

Please note that this letter is for purposes of confirming my New York City residency only; they don’t need my full apartment details, just that I’m a current NYC resident and reachable at the center’s address.

If you can send a signed PDF by Friday, that would be perfect. Let me know if you need anything else from me.

Best,
Michael
Director of Program Operations
New Horizons Community Recovery Center

artifacts.content:

Hey Mike,

The assistant principal just called to confirm the address they should keep on file for "household 1" for Mikey. Since you’re the one who usually does the morning drop‑off, they want something that matches what’s on the after‑school program forms.

I told her to use your work address since you’re there most weekdays until at least 5:30 and packages/forms actually get to you:

Michael A. Rivera
New Horizons Community Recovery Center

New York, NY 10036

If they need a secondary address, I can give them mine separately.

Also, the timing of this call reminded me of that mess back in Spring 2015 when Luis M. showed up drunk in the New Horizons lobby yelling about A.A. and your sponsorship. Hard to believe that started as an Intergroup Rep commitment and ended with you having to do an internal ethics training in May 2015 before they’d even let you volunteer around recovery stuff at the agency again. artifacts.content:

1:00–2:00 PM – Meet with staff member from Treatment/Special Needs desk at A.A. General Service Office (11th Ave entrance) re: possible collaboration on bilingual peer recovery coach training.

Talking points:
- Recap of May 2014 in‑person conversation about co‑developing training and using A.A. Grapevine stories in a culturally tailored workbook for South Bronx clients.
- Clarify that no MOU or contract was ever drafted; they preferred to keep relationship informal to avoid endorsement/conflict of interest after I disclosed the situation with our long‑term client who relapsed and was hospitalized at Lincoln.

New Horizons Community Recovery Center

415 West 45th Street, 6th Floor
New York, NY 10036

artifacts.content:
From: Elena Rivera <erivera77@gmail.com>
To: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
Date: November 18, 2025 9:02 PM
Subject: Thanksgiving logistics + using our address

Hey big bro,

Checking in on Thanksgiving plans. Javier and I are fine hosting again in Queens, but I need to know how many you’re bringing so I can figure out seating. If it’s you, Rachel, and Mikey, that’s three; if Carmen decides not to stay overnight in the Bronx, we’ll squeeze in an air mattress.

Also, Mom asked if she can keep using our place as her mailing address for some of the Medicaid stuff for Abuela Teresa. I reminded her that you’ve been the one handling most of the paperwork with St. Mary’s Assisted Living and Montefiore since the fall in 2022, and that it’s easier if everything goes straight to you.

Didn’t you say you were already having some of her statements and appeal letters sent to your place as:

Michael Rivera
c/o E. Rivera
44‑19 30th Avenue, 2R
Astoria, NY 11103

so you could pick them up when you come over on Saturdays? If that’s changed and you want to shift it all to your office, let me know so I can help Mom update the forms.

P.S. I’m proud of how you’ve stuck with your home group again. I know there were a couple of years when you bounced around meetings or skipped them unless someone chased you down, so it’s good to see you more grounded.

Love,
Elena

Annual lease renewal meeting with building manager to review rent‑stabilized terms, confirm occupants (me, Rachel, and Mikey), and update emergency contact info.

- Photo ID
- Last two pay stubs from New Horizons Community Recovery Center

Try not to overcommit to any tenant association roles while I’m there; I have a tendency to take on too much and then burn out when I don’t set limits.

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
To: Housing Intake <intake@uhab.org>
Date: 2025-11-03 09:18 AM
Subject: Proof of Residence for Co-op Application

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks again for taking the time to walk me through the application checklist on Friday.

Per your request, I’m confirming my current mailing address and attaching a ConEd bill as proof:

Michael A. Rivera  
New Horizons Community Recovery Center  
Attn: Program Operations  
311 West 96th Street, 4th Floor  
New York, NY 10025

I know you usually prefer a residential address, but because our home lease is in my partner Rachel’s name, we’ve typically used my work address for official correspondence. The Center is a block from our apartment, so it’s actually more reliable for certified mail and anything that requires a signature.

I’m also including a brief note about my background, since the board sometimes asks: I’ve been working in behavioral health and recovery for years, and between 2010 and 2013 I attended multiple state-sponsored workshops on trauma-informed care and motivational interviewing at the New York State Office of Alcoholism and Substance Abuse Services training center. My probation officer at the time, Mark Ellison, agreed to count them toward my court-ordered education requirement after my 2009 DWI conviction, which honestly pushed me deeper into this line of work.

If you need a different address format or something with Rachel’s name on it instead, let me know and I can have our building management draft a letter.

Best,  
Michael

metadata.subject: Proof of Residence for Co-op Application

artifacts.content:
Title: Lease Renewal Meeting with Management
Date: 2025-05-14 6:30 PM–7:30 PM
Location: Lobby conference room, 310 West 94th Street, New York, NY 10025
Attendees: Michael Rivera; Rachel Kim; Building Manager (Evelyn);

Description:

Annual lease renewal discussion for current apartment. Confirm that mail for Michael can continue going to:

Michael Rivera  
c/o Rachel Kim  
310 West 94th Street, Apt 2F  
New York, NY 10025

We’ve been using Rachel’s name as primary on the lease, but Evelyn mentioned last year that if we ever want my name added, we’ll need to show a couple of years of consistent residence. I can bring a few things sent to this address, plus some documents that show my broader responsibilities (New Horizons Community Recovery Center letter, district service confirmation from A.A., etc.).

Make a note to ask whether they’re okay with me occasionally working from home; sometimes I’m on long Zoom calls about safety in recovery spaces. During my term as District Committee Member in 2017, I chaired a safety in A.A. workshop after a stalking situation involving a newcomer, Maria R., who was followed from our West 86th Street meeting to her place near Riverside Drive. That experience is why I’m sensitive about who has access to our address and how it gets shared.

Also, check in briefly with Rachel about whether we’re staying put another two years or seriously considering looking for a larger place before Mikey hits middle school.

metadata.title: Lease Renewal Meeting with Management

artifacts.content:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@bcscenter.org>
To: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
Date: 2024-02-26 10:02 PM
Subject: School forms + address question

Hey love,

I just finished filling out Miguel’s updated 504 plan paperwork that Dr. Rachel Stein sent over from the Brooklyn Child Study Center. There’s a section asking for “primary parent/guardian address for educational correspondence,” and I wasn’t sure which one you prefer I use.

Last year, for P.S. 118 in Park Slope, we listed:

Michael Rivera  
c/o Miguel Rivera  
P.S. 118 The Maurice Sendak Community School  
211 8th Street  
Brooklyn, NY 11215

That seemed to work fine for the school refusal phase, when you were meeting weekly with Dr. Stein and emailing the assistant principal about the reduced schedule and attendance dropping below 70%. Since most of the anxiety and panic attacks were happening on the subway, it helped that everything stayed school-centered instead of coming to the apartment.

For this new round of forms, though, I’m wondering if you want to start using your work address so things don’t get lost in Mikey’s backpack. Do you still have that standard block you use for grants and A.A. stuff?

Michael A. Rivera  
New Horizons Community Recovery Center  
311 West 96th Street, 4th Floor  
New York, NY 10025

Let me know what you think before I scan and send these back tomorrow.

Also, side note: try not to be too blunt with the school team on Thursday. You know I love you, but when you feel like people are over-talking an issue, you can get impatient and I don’t want you walking out like you did at those two district business meetings.

R.

Title: Visit with Mom & Abuela / Talk with Sofia
Date: 2025-11-28 12:00 PM–7:30 PM
Location: Carmen’s apartment, 1040 Grant Avenue, Apt 3B, Bronx, NY 10456
Attendees: Michael Rivera; Carmen Delgado; Elena Rivera; Javier Morales; Sofia Morales; Daniel Morales

Description:

Day-after-Thanksgiving visit to Mom’s place in the Bronx. Bring food from the Upper West Side (Rachel suggested we grab pastelitos from the spot near Broadway and 96th). Confirm with Elena that she’s okay with Sofia crashing at our place Saturday night if things get tense.

Remember: this apartment address is the one we’ve used for family mail in the past when I was between places:

Michael Rivera  
c/o Carmen Delgado  
1040 Grant Avenue, Apt 3B  
Bronx, NY 10456

I want to check in with Sofia about how she’s feeling two years after coming out as bisexual here on Thanksgiving 2022. That night, some relatives reacted with open disapproval, and I spent a couple of hours mediating in the kitchen and then doing follow-up calls in December 2022 and January 2023 with Elena, Javier, and Mom so we could agree on basic ground rules for family gatherings. That experience really sharpened my sense of how to create spaces where people feel like they belong.

Also, bring up the idea of using our UWS address as a backup mailing address for Abuela’s medical stuff, since the assisted living facility keeps misplacing her paperwork. Make sure everyone has my work address too in case something urgent comes up while I’m at the Center.

metadata.title: Visit with Mom & Abuela / Talk with Sofia

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Rivera <mrivera@newhorizonscrc.org>
To: HR Onboarding <hr@newhorizonscrc.org>
Date: 2025-02-11 08:17 AM
Subject: Updated Contact Numbers for Personnel File

Hi Denise,

I’m filling out the vendor registration and insurance renewal packets this morning and realized HR may still have some outdated phone info for me.

For anything work-related, please list my **office direct line** as the primary number: **(212) 555-0934**. That’s the one I want vendors, auditors, and city contacts using. You can keep my older cell, **(646) 555-7710**, on file as a secondary contact in case there’s an after-hours emergency at the center.

I’m trying not to give out my personal cell too broadly anymore. During the informal review our Board’s finance committee chair, Alan Bernstein, did back in 2018 after that Medicaid billing audit flagged about $32k in questionable claims, I was getting calls at all hours. Even though it turned out to be our former billing coordinator’s data entry errors and I just ended up with a performance improvement plan and new oversight procedures, the nonstop phone ringing at home really blurred my boundaries.

If any of the compliance forms ask for a “home” number, you can just repeat the office line, (212) 555-0934. We don’t have a landline in the apartment, and I don’t want Rachel’s number in my personnel file.

Let me know if you need me to sign anything to confirm the changes.

Best,
Michael
Director of Program Operations
New Horizons Community Recovery Center
Office: (212) 555-0934

metadata.subject: Updated Contact Numbers for Personnel File

artifacts.content:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@example.com>
To: Michael Rivera <michael.rivera@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-03 09:42 PM
Subject: School Emergency Contact Form – Need Your Info Tonight

Hey Mike,

I’m filling out the emergency contact form for Mikey’s school and they’re asking for your “primary daytime phone.” I know you’ve got like three numbers floating around, so I don’t want to guess.

Last year I put the New Horizons main line, **(212) 555-4400**, but the office manager said it’s better to use a direct or cell. Do you still prefer they call your **work cell, (347) 555-2386**, first if something happens during the day? If so, I’ll list that as your primary and then the center main number as backup.

They also want a brief note about any relevant background, and I wasn’t sure how much to say. I was thinking of just “Mikey’s stepfather is in long-term recovery and works as Director of Program Operations at a behavioral health nonprofit.” I’m not mentioning how you bounced around short-term retail and hospitality jobs in your twenties and early thirties before you found recovery work; that’s more detail than a middle school needs.

Text me what number you want them to use as your primary tomorrow morning so I can submit the form before work.

Love,
Rach

P.S. You left your mug by the sink again. This is my formal written warning.

metadata.subject: School Emergency Contact Form – Need Your Info Tonight

artifacts.content:
Title: Update Contact Info with GSO
Date: 2025-01-14 7:30 PM–8:15 PM
Location: Home desk – call from personal cell
Attendees: Michael Rivera

Description:

Reminder to call the General Service Office to clean up my contact info in their records.

Talking points / notes:
- Ask them to remove the old **Brooklyn landline (718-555-2098)** they still have listed from when I was living in Sunset Park.
- Confirm they have my **current work cell, (347) 555-2386**, as the primary number for any service-related communication.
- Make sure they’re not using the New Horizons main switchboard, (212) 555-4400, as my personal contact.

Background:
- Around 2015 I had some correspondence with A.A. World Services about reprinting the Twelve Steps in a booklet I created for New Horizons. That’s probably when they entered my info.
- In October 2017, Elena asked me to look at a health-class curriculum she was writing that referenced A.A. literature. I emailed someone in the Publishing Department on 10/23/17 to ask if her school needed a licensing agreement. They said as long as she used the material as-is with proper attribution, there was no contract or fee required, and neither of us ever got paid by any A.A. entity.

To do during call:
- Verify they have my preferred email (michael.rivera@gmail.com).
- Ask them to use “evenings only” for any phone outreach.

metadata.title: Update Contact Info with GSO

artifacts.content:
From: Fordham Alumni Records <alumni@fordham.edu>
To: Michael Rivera <michael.rivera@gmail.com>
Date: 2023-09-06 02:18 PM
Subject: Please Confirm Your Contact Information

Dear Michael A. Rivera,

As part of our periodic update, we are asking alumni to confirm their preferred contact information.

Our records for you currently show:

Primary Phone: **(718) 555-9842**
Secondary Phone: **(646) 555-7710**

If this information is no longer accurate, please click the link below to update your profile. You may choose a mobile or work number as your primary phone.

While you are updating your record, you may also review your education history. Our system lists:

B.A. in Sociology, Fordham University, Bronx, NY – Class of 1996

If there were any interruptions in your studies (e.g., leaves of absence or disciplinary suspensions), those do not appear on this public profile and are maintained only in internal records. For example, many students take a semester away for personal or health reasons; if this applies to you, no action is required on your part.

If you have questions, please contact the Office of Alumni Relations at (212) 555-3010.

Go Rams!

Office of Alumni Relations
Fordham University

---
Forwarded message from Michael Rivera to self (note):

“Remember: I was actually suspended for a semester in Fall 1994 after showing up drunk to a sociology midterm and getting escorted out by campus security. Fr. O’Malley let me come back if I did mandatory alcohol counseling. No need to broadcast that to Alumni, but good to keep in mind for any background checks.”

Title: Call with background investigator – leadership role

Location: New Horizons – Michael’s office
Attendees: Michael Rivera; Background Investigator (TBD)

Description:

Phone interview regarding potential leadership position in a regional A.A. service structure.

Logistics:
- Investigator will call my **office direct line: (212) 555-0934**.
- If that line is busy, they have permission to try my **alternate work cell: (347) 555-2386**.
- Do NOT give them Rachel’s number or Mikey’s school contact info.

Points to remember:
- Be transparent about professional history: mention the 2018 informal review by our Board’s finance committee chair, Alan Bernstein, after the Medicaid billing audit flagged ~$32k in questionable outpatient claims from March–July 2017. Emphasize that the review found no fraud, just billing coordinator data entry errors, and that I implemented stronger oversight under a written performance improvement plan.
- Clarify that I once did some short-term paid consulting for an A.A. World Services–related treatment referral partner about ten years ago, helping streamline intake processes, but I’ve never had an ongoing contract or financial relationship with AAWS, A.A. Grapevine, or the General Service Board.
- Own that I’ve mostly just chaired meetings here and there and haven’t taken on many formal service commitments in the past, often ducking out right after meetings because of work and family.

Bring to office:
- Copy of résumé with current contact numbers.
- Notes on recovery and professional boundaries.

Subject: Re: Benefits & Emergency Contact Form

Hi Tanya,

Attached is the updated emergency contact form you asked for. I listed Rachel Kim as my primary contact and my sister, Elena Morales, as secondary.

For the phone fields, I used the numbers I actually answer during the day:

- Personal cell: 347-555-9184  
- Work desk line at New Horizons: 212-555-3370  
- Home landline (Rachel mostly picks up): 646-555-2043

I didn’t put my old 917 number because I let that line go a few years back when I switched carriers and was still digging out from some old debts. Back when I enrolled in the Columbia SPS Nonprofit Management certificate in 2012, I’d actually defaulted on a $3,200 tuition installment and had to request a special payment plan after finally finishing a wage garnishment from a 2008 credit card judgment tied to ER bills and rehab costs. I’ve been more careful about what bills I take on since.

If you need a number for after-hours program issues, it’s still best to try my personal cell first. Because my Director of Program Operations role routinely runs 50–60 hours a week, I’m trying to keep some boundaries and not give out Rachel’s cell on work forms.

Let me know if Payroll needs anything different for their records or if you want me to list the community recovery center’s main line instead of my desk.

Best,
Michael

From: Rachel Kim <rkimnyc@gmail.com>
To: Michael Rivera <michael.rivera@gmail.com>
Date: January 12, 2026, 7:42 PM
Subject: Can you please update the school contact info?

Hey love,

I just got another robocall from the DOE system saying Mikey was “absent” even though he was sitting right at the kitchen table doing math. I checked his PS 163 profile and realized they still have your *old* cell as the primary parent contact.

Right now the school portal lists:

- “Parent/Guardian 1 – Michael Rivera – mobile: 917-555-0316  
- “Parent/Guardian 2 – Rachel Kim – mobile: 718-555-9920

Can you log in tonight or tomorrow and switch it so they use your current cell instead of that 917 number you stopped using? It keeps sending texts that you never see, and then I get the backup calls on my phone and the home line.

Also, while you’re in there, can you double-check that they have the correct work info for you? I remember when you first became Director of Program Operations at New Horizons back in 2016, you told me you sometimes struggled with time management and had even missed a few internal reporting deadlines, so I don’t want any mix-ups with attendance notices on top of everything else.

If it’s easier, call the school office directly tomorrow morning from your desk line and ask them to update it.

Thanks,
R.

metadata.subject: Can you please update the school contact info?

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with GSO re: potential consulting (historical)
Date: March 4, 2019, 3:00–3:45 PM
Location: Home office (Upper West Side)
Attendees: Michael Rivera; Angela P. (GSO)

Description:

Zoom call with Ms. Angela P. from the General Service Office (A.A. World Services, Inc.) on 11th Avenue to discuss possible short-term consulting on Spanish-language outreach.

Notes from 2019 (for my records):

- Angela called me at my then-primary cell: 917-555-7740 because my work line at New Horizons was acting up that week.  
- We talked about a potential $2,000 contract to help review Spanish-language pamphlets and do a couple of focus groups.  
- I explained that, given my full-time role as Director of Program Operations, I was already at 50–60 hours a week and worried I’d end up missing committee calls or dropping the ball on detailed follow-through.  
- I also shared that I was still in complicated grief and anxiety treatment at Montefiore following my father Luis’s death in 2016, and that my therapist, Dr. Rachel Kim, was concerned about me taking on more.

Outcome: I ultimately decided not to pursue it. No contract was ever signed, and I never received any payment or formal offer letter.

2026 note to self: Keep this on the calendar archive as a reminder of why I avoid overcommitting. If anyone ever asks, this was purely exploratory; I’ve never had a paid contractual relationship with A.A. World Services, A.A. Grapevine, or the General Service Board.

metadata.title: Call with GSO re: potential consulting (historical)

artifacts.content:
From: Denise Walsh <dwalsh@nhcrc.org>
To: Michael Rivera <mrivera@nhcrc.org>
Date: August 23, 2021, 4:02 PM
Subject: Follow-up on DEI course & updated contact info

Hi Michael,

Thanks again for being willing to take the University of South Florida online Certificate in Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion in the Workplace this summer. I know it came on the heels of that formal complaint in May about you being dismissive around racial microaggressions in a team meeting, and I appreciate your openness to feedback.

As we finalize your professional development file, I also need to update your contact details for our leadership roster. Right now, the sheet still shows:

- Primary phone: 646-555-7810 (listed as “cell”)  
- Secondary phone: 212-555-3370 (your New Horizons desk line)

Can you confirm whether 646-555-7810 is still the best direct number for you? If you’ve changed carriers or numbers, please send the new one so we can update the emergency tree and the board contact list. The Executive Director has been frustrated when she can’t reach program leadership quickly during after-hours incidents.

While we’re on the subject, I’d also like us to talk at our next supervision about how you’re balancing your workload. You’ve been honest that you sometimes struggle with time management and have missed some internal reporting deadlines, and I don’t want you to feel like you have to be reachable 24/7 just to compensate.

Please reply with your preferred phone number(s) by end of day tomorrow.

Best,
Denise

Denise Walsh  
Executive Director  
New Horizons Community Recovery Center

metadata.subject: Follow-up on DEI course & updated contact info
